pkg/framework/entutils/entcursor.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that attaches a Cursor(ctx, *pagination.Cursor) method to every Ent query type whose schema has a created_at field, enabling stable cursor pagination ordered by (created_at ASC, id ASC). Registered in openmeter/ent/entc.go; takes effect only after make generate.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: The Extension embeds entc.DefaultExtension, implements Templates() returning a gen.MustParse'd template loaded via //go:embed cursor.tpl, and exposes New(). This is the only supported way to add generated methods here.

patterns.name: created_at gate in template
patterns.description: The template only emits Cursor(...) for nodes that have a field named created_at; schemas without it get no method.

patterns.name: Deterministic ordering created_at ASC, id ASC
patterns.description: The generated method always appends ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC; callers must not add conflicting order clauses before calling .Cursor().

patterns.name: CAST(id AS TEXT) for cursor comparison
patterns.description: The cursor WHERE clause casts id to TEXT for string comparison so it works with ULID/UUID IDs. Integer-ID schemas break this assumption (lexicographic, not numeric).
patterns.example: b.WriteString("CAST("); b.WriteString(s.C("id")); b.WriteString(" AS TEXT) > "); b.Args(cursor.ID)

patterns.name: Non-nil Items on empty result
patterns.description: Generated code initialises items to an empty slice when nil so JSON serialises as [] not null.

key_file_guides.role: Go template generating the Cursor method on every qualifying Ent query type at go generate time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Rendered once per $.Nodes entry — changes affect ALL entities with created_at. The CAST assumes string IDs; numeric-ID schemas produce wrong results silently.

key_file_guides.role: Registers the template as an Ent extension via entc.Extension. Must be referenced in openmeter/ent/entc.go to take effect.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If not listed in the entc.Generate call, no cursor methods are generated despite this code existing.

key_file_guides.role: Integration test using testutils.InitPostgresDB; validates first-page, next-page, empty-result, and invalid-cursor paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Requires POSTGRES_HOST=127.0.0.1 and -tags=dynamic; uses the ent1 test schema, not production.

anti_patterns: Adding ORDER BY before calling .Cursor() — the template appends its own order, making combined ordering undefined
Using this extension on a schema with an integer ID — CAST-to-TEXT gives lexicographic, not numeric, ordering
Editing cursor.tpl without running make generate — generated methods in openmeter/ent/db/ go stale
Calling .Cursor() with a zero-value pagination.Cursor{} — cursor.Validate() returns an error

decisions.decision: Composite (created_at, id) cursor key instead of offset pagination
decisions.rationale: Offset pagination drifts under concurrent inserts; a (time, id) tuple is stable and avoids COUNT(*) on large tables.

decisions.decision: Gate generation on created_at presence rather than an annotation
decisions.rationale: All domain entities use the TimeMixin that adds created_at, so the gate is a zero-config convention.

code_examples.scenario: Using the generated Cursor method to paginate an Ent query

pkg/framework/entutils/entdriver.purpose: Thin wrapper pairing an entDialectSQL.Driver with an entdb.Client into a single EntPostgresDriver struct — the canonical way to construct and close Ent + Postgres connections in all binaries and tests. Clone() yields isolated clients sharing one connection (used in tests).

patterns.name: NewEntPostgresDriver from *sql.DB
patterns.description: Always construct via NewEntPostgresDriver(db *sql.DB) — never instantiate EntPostgresDriver directly — so entDialectSQL.OpenDB(dialect.Postgres, db) wrapping is applied consistently.

patterns.name: Clone for isolated clients sharing one connection
patterns.description: Clone() creates a second EntPostgresDriver sharing the underlying *sql.DB but with its own entdb.Client; used in tests to give each test an isolated client.

patterns.name: Symmetric Close: client first, driver second
patterns.description: Close() closes the entdb.Client before the entDialectSQL.Driver. Reversing or skipping the client close leaks Ent connection state.

key_file_guides.role: Sole file — defines EntPostgresDriver and NewEntPostgresDriver. Imported by app/common for wiring and openmeter/testutils for test DB setup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Client() returns the raw *entdb.Client; never call Close() on it separately after handing it to Wire — EntPostgresDriver.Close() owns the lifecycle.

anti_patterns: Constructing entdb.NewClient directly in domain or app code instead of going through EntPostgresDriver
Calling driver.Client().Close() independently — EntPostgresDriver.Close() already does this; double-closing errors
Passing *entdb.Client across binary boundaries — the client is not serialisable; use Kafka or HTTP

decisions.decision: Bundle driver and client into one struct
decisions.rationale: Ent requires both to be closed in the right order; bundling prevents callers from forgetting one half.

pkg/framework/entutils/entexpose.purpose: Ent code-generation extension that injects GetConfig(), HijackTx(), and NewTxClientFromRawConfig() into each generated db package, enabling the shared cross-client transaction mechanism used by entutils.TransactingRepo — the foundation of the ctx-propagated transaction pattern across all domain adapters.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: entexpose.go implements entc.Extension with Templates() returning a gen.MustParse'd template from expose.tpl. Must be registered in openmeter/ent/entc.go.

patterns.name: HijackTx for cross-client transaction sharing
patterns.description: HijackTx(ctx, opts) starts a SQL transaction on the client's driver and returns (ctx, *RawEntConfig, *ExposedTxDriver, error). The RawEntConfig is passed to NewTxClientFromRawConfig in a second client to bind it to the same transaction.

patterns.name: ExposedTxDriver implements Transactable with savepoints
patterns.description: ExposedTxDriver exposes Rollback, Commit, SavePoint, RollbackTo, Release — all SAVEPOINT SQL issued via ExecContext on the txDriver. This lets entutils.TransactingRepo nest transactions.

patterns.name: NewTxClientFromRawConfig reconstructs a Tx with empty hooks/inters
patterns.description: Deliberately creates a Tx with empty hooks and inters structs — meaning Ent on-rollback/on-commit hooks are ignored for shared transactions (per the template TODO).
patterns.example: config := config{driver: cfg.Driver, debug: cfg.Debug, log: cfg.Log, hooks: &hooks{}, inters: &inters{}}

key_file_guides.role: Core template generating GetConfig, HijackTx, NewTxClientFromRawConfig, and ExposedTxDriver into the db package. Changes affect ALL generated Ent packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tx.onRollback and Tx.onCommit hooks are silently dropped in shared transactions — do not rely on Ent commit/rollback hooks in shared-tx flows.

key_file_guides.role: Extension registration — consumed by entc.Generate in openmeter/ent/entc.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with pkg/framework/entutils types (RawEntConfig, Transactable) — the template references them by name.

anti_patterns: Starting a transaction with HijackTx on a client that already has a txDriver — the template returns an error for nested HijackTx
Relying on Ent commit/rollback hooks in shared-transaction paths — NewTxClientFromRawConfig initialises empty hooks
Editing expose.tpl without regenerating openmeter/ent/db/ via make generate

decisions.decision: Expose raw txDriver internals via code generation rather than a public Ent API
decisions.rationale: Ent has no first-class API for sharing a transaction across multiple Client instances; hijacking the internal txDriver is the only way without forking Ent.

pkg/framework/entutils/entmixinaccessor.purpose: Ent code-generation extension generating typed GetXxx() getters for every field (including ID) added to an entity schema via a mixin, enabling interface-based access to mixin fields (namespace, timestamps, ID) without reflection or direct struct access.

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: entmixinaccessor.go registers mixinaccessor.tpl via entc.DefaultExtension + Templates(). Register in openmeter/ent/entc.go.

patterns.name: MixedIn position gate
patterns.description: Getters are generated only for fields where $f.Position.MixedIn is true (contributed by a mixin); fields defined directly on the schema get none.

patterns.name: Nillable fields get pointer return type
patterns.description: If a mixin field is Nillable, the getter returns *<Type> matching the struct field's actual type.
patterns.example: func (e *{{ $n.Name }}) Get{{ $f.StructField }}() {{ if $f.Nillable }}*{{ end }}{{ $f.Type }} { return e.{{ $f.StructField }} }

patterns.name: ID getter templated separately
patterns.description: The ID field is not in $n.Fields; it is accessed via $n.ID and gets GetID() only when $n.ID.Position.MixedIn is true.
patterns.example: {{- if and $n.ID $n.ID.Position $n.ID.Position.MixedIn }} func (e *{{ $n.Name }}) GetID() {{ $n.ID.Type }} { return e.ID } {{- end }}

key_file_guides.role: Generates typed getters for mixin-injected fields on every entity, enabling adapters to access e.g. entity.GetNamespace() via a shared interface without type-asserting.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only mixin fields get getters. A field added directly to a schema needs a manual getter or must be moved to a mixin for interface compliance.

key_file_guides.role: Extension registration — mounts the template into Ent codegen.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must be included in the entc.Generate extension list to have any effect.

anti_patterns: Accessing mixin fields like namespace via direct struct access in interface-based adapter code — use the generated GetNamespace() getter
Defining a field directly on a schema when it should be a mixin contribution — direct fields get no getters

decisions.decision: Generate getters only for mixin fields, not all fields
decisions.rationale: Mixin fields (namespace, timestamps, ID) are shared across entities for generic adapter interfaces; getters for every field would bloat generated code.

pkg/framework/entutils/entpaginate.purpose: Ent code-generation extension attaching a Paginate(ctx, pagination.Page) method to every Ent query type — offset-based pagination with automatic COUNT(*) and a guaranteed non-nil Items slice. Generated for ALL entity types unconditionally (unlike entcursor, which gates on created_at).

patterns.name: Ent Extension + embedded template
patterns.description: paginate.go registers paginate.tpl following the same pattern as all entutils extensions.

patterns.name: Paginate generated for ALL nodes (no gate)
patterns.description: Unlike entcursor, the template generates Paginate(...) for every entity regardless of fields present.

patterns.name: Clone for COUNT, original for data
patterns.description: The template clones the query for COUNT(*) (clearing fields and order) then runs the data query on the original. Callers must not mutate the query after passing it to Paginate.

patterns.name: Zero-value Page returns all items
patterns.description: If page.IsZero(), limit=totalCount and offset=0 — every item is returned with TotalCount populated.

patterns.name: Non-nil Items on empty result
patterns.description: Returns make([]*Node, 0) when count==0 so JSON serialises as [] not null.

patterns.name: Static Paginator interface type-check
patterns.description: Each generated Paginate is validated against pagination.Paginator[*Node] via a compile-time var _ = assertion.

key_file_guides.role: Generates Paginate(ctx, pagination.Page) on every Ent query type. The two-query pattern (COUNT + data) means 2 DB round trips per call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Ordering applied before .Paginate() is preserved for the data query but stripped for COUNT. Apply ordering before calling Paginate, not after.
Must be registered in openmeter/ent/entc.go alongside the other entutils extensions.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests covering first page, ordering, filtering, multi-page, empty page, and zero-page (return all) against real Postgres.

anti_patterns: Applying .Limit() or .Offset() before .Paginate() — the template resets both to 0 on entry, discarding caller values
Using Paginate for large unbounded exports — the zero-page path fetches ALL rows into memory; use entcursor instead
Relying on COUNT accuracy under high concurrency — the two-query pattern has a TOCTOU gap

decisions.decision: Two-query approach (COUNT + data) rather than a SQL window function
decisions.rationale: Ent's query builder exposes no window-function API; clone-and-count is the simplest correct approach with the existing abstraction.
Offset pagination requires no specific schema field (unlike cursor's created_at), so there is no meaningful gate.

code_examples.scenario: Offset-paginated list of entities with ordering and filtering
